snippet.title: India’s Chip Making Plans In The Fast Lane; First Chip To Be Out By Dec. 2024 | Watch | In Focus
snippet.description:
Watch as Union Minister for Communications, Electronics & Information Technology Ashwini Vaishnaw talks about crucial semiconductor chip investment and manufacturing deals signed during Prime Minister's state visit to the U.S.A . 

PM's meetings with Micron President & CEO Sanjay Mehrotra & Gary E. Dickerson, CEO Applied Materials have successfully attracted investment promises. This was especially crucial as despite India’s big policy push India’s semiconductor chip manufacturing plans are yet to yield results a year and a half later.

snippet.title: They're on a REVENGE Tour!
snippet.description:
In Episode 7, "The Revenge," the stage is set for an epic showdown as North Side squares off against their rivals, Northrop. This game carries an added layer of intensity, fueled by the bitter memory of their previous encounter. In a COVID ridden matchup, Northrop emerged victorious, taking advantage of a limited North Side squad missing half its players to COVID restrictions. What stung even more was Northrop's post-victory celebration at North Side, leaving a sour taste with the North Side legends.

Now, with the opportunity for redemption in their sights, North Side is on a mission to settle the score, determined to rewrite the narrative and avenge their previous loss. With their season record at stake, North Side is hungry to climb to an impressive 6-2, showcasing their resilience and fighting spirit.

This episode promises high-stakes drama, as North Side seeks to turn the tables on Northrop and secure the ultimate revenge victory. Join us as we witness the passion, the rivalry, and the unwavering commitment of these young athletes in their quest for redemption in this thrilling chapter of "The New North."

In a COVID ridden matchup, Northrop emerged victorious, taking advantage of a limited North Side squad missing half its players to COVID restrictions. US semiconductor toolmaker Applied Materials will invest $400 million (roughly Rs. 3,300 crore) over four years in a new engineering centre in India, the company said on Thursday.

Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with the company's CEO Gary Dickerson in Washington on Wednesday and invited Applied to strengthen the chip industry in the country.

Applied's investment is among a flurry of announcements this week including General Electric's deal to jointly produce jet engines for the military with state-owned Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd and data storage chipmaker Micron's $825 million (roughly Rs. 6,760 crore) investment to build a new factory in India.

Modi also met Tesla CEO Elon Musk after which the automaker's top boss said the company will try to be in India "as soon as humanly possible."

The new center is expected to be located near the company's existing facility in Bengaluru and is likely to support more than $2 billion (roughly Rs. 16,400 crore) of planned investments and create 500 new advanced engineering jobs, the company said.

Applied currently operates across six sites in India and works closely with the Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, and the Indian Institute of Technology in Mumbai, two of the country's prestigious institutions.

US chipmaker Micron also announced Thursday it would invest up to $825 million (roughly Rs. 6,760 crore) in a new chip assembly and test facility in Gujarat, India, its first factory in the country.

Micron said that with support from the Indian central government and from the state of Gujarat, the total investment in the facility will be $2.75 billion (roughly Rs. 20,500 crores). Of that total, 50 percent will come from the Indian central government and 20 percent from the state of Gujarat.

Micron said construction of the new facility in Gujarat is expected to begin in 2023 and the first phase of the project will be operational in late 2024. 

© Thomson Reuters 2023

snippet.title: Man who lived in world-famous Connecticut haunted house tells of spooky experiences
snippet.description:
When Jim Post and his wife Jackie rented the top two floors of a big Southington colonial in 1995, they had no idea of its reputation as a haunted house or of the history that would one day make it the subject of a horror film called, "The Haunting in Connecticut."

As fate would have it, they found out on Halloween, when Post asked the first-floor tenant if he wanted to share trick-or-treating duties.

The man replied, "We don't do Halloween anymore. It got too weird, people would come from all over, and they would line up down the sidewalk and down the road, wanting to get in."

snippet.title: Prospect for Progress - How Can the U.S. & China Best Use the Period Ahead?
snippet.description:
Silicon Valley, January 12, 2023 — Asia Society Northern California hosts its fifth annual U.S. & China Conference: The Future of U.S. & China: Moving Towards the Edge. This full-day event features leaders in business and trade, experts on China, and journalists exploring the complex and evolving relationship between these two global powers.
 
In the day’s 8th session, Gary Rieschel, Chair at Asia Society Northern California and Founding Managing Partner of Qiming, leads us in conversation with panelists Danny Russel, Vice President for International Security and Diplomacy at the Asia Society Policy Institute and Jerry Yang, Founding Partner of AME Cloud Ventures and Co-Founder of Yahoo! Inc. Together, they explore the potential of improving U.S.-China relations through the forging of a mutual understanding of cultures.
 
Also discussed – the future of Taiwan and mainland China, the cyclical nature of international relationships, and the importance of improved communication.
 
This event provides a diverse range of perspectives on various topics, including the emergence of Asia as a global power, the shifting dynamics of international trade, China's domestic politics, the balance between competition and cooperation in finance, and how young professionals are navigating a changed China.
 
Thank you to our Lead and Impact Sponsors: Pang and Yum 2002 Trust, Kenson Ventures, Tencent

snippet.title: He was the BEST PLAYER in the 2019 Recruiting Class... Now He Plays for a COMMUNITY College...
snippet.description:
Being born and raised on the jersey shore, I love covering New Jersey football products on this channel. New Jersey has always been a hotbed for recruiting and we have developed players like Minkah Fitzpatrick, Rashan Gary, Jonathan Taylor, and many more. There are always a handful of four-star recruits and sometimes a handful of five-star recruits coming out every year. The last five-star player coming out of the state is former Alabama commit Antonio Alfano back in 2019 who has gone on to have a tumultuous college football career so far. He has yet to play a down since 2018 and has spent time with Alabama, Colorado, and Independence community college and will have one last shot at college football at Lackawanna Community college in Scranton Pennsylvania. Alfano is not a simple bust story. He has dealt with personal loss, medical issues, and possibly some academic issues. But that has not stopped him from continuing to put his head down and continuing to work to achieve his dream of playing in college and hopefully the NFL. This is a story of determination and never giving up, this is the story of Antonio Alfano who truly has one last chance.

snippet.title: “Will never forget this…” Young member of India diaspora elated as he gets autograph from PM Modi
snippet.description:
PM Modi landed in Washington DC, United States on June 21 for historic State Visit to US. He received an electrifying welcome from the Indian Diaspora upon his arrival at Hotel Intercontinental Willard. During his interaction with Indian Americans, PM Modi signed an autograph for a child. It was a “memorable moment” as the child received a high-five from Indian PM. PM Modi was accompanied by External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar. On June 22, PM Modi will be accorded a ceremonial Guard of Honour at the White House. PM Modi will be hosted by US President Joe Biden and First Lady Jill Biden. He will also address a Joint Session of the US Congress for the second time.

snippet.title: Babe Didrikson Zaharias Swing Analysis: Greatest Woman Golfer Ever?
snippet.description:
I confess I didn’t know a whole lot about the Babe until I read up on her, and I think anyone would admit that her story is pretty incredible. I forgot to mention it in the video, but she is the only woman ever to make a cut in a men’s Tour event, and she did it 3 straight times in 1945, and is also still the only woman to ever qualify for a PGA Tour event as opposed to receiving a sponsor’s exemption. Her record as an amateur includes winning 17 straight events, a feat never matched, and won the U.S. Women’s Amateur in 1946 and the British Women’s Amateur in 1947, this after winning the Women’s Western Open (a major 3 times from 1942 to She turned pro in 1948 and added to her 4 majors she won as an amateur by winning the U.S. Women’s Open. She would win 5 more majors and a total of 48 professional wins before succumbing to cancer in 1956 at age 45. She won the 1954 U.S. Women’s Open wearing a colostomy bag. Think about that for a moment.
 
Her swing is the most aggressive woman’s swing I’ve ever seen. Her sequencing is amazing, and her pelvic movement is unlike any other woman (other than Mickey Wright and Annika Sorenstam). She excelled at just about every sport including basketball and baseball, and her athleticism was showcased in the 1932 Olympics where she won 2 gold and 1 silver medal in the hurdles, high jump and javelin throw, which indicates she had strength, speed, and technique, all of which she applied to golf. I compare her to Hogan, who I think would have won more majors than even Nicklaus had he not been so badly injured and am sure she would have continued to win had her career not been ended by cancer.

snippet.title: First Made-In-India Chip To Come Out By December 2024: Ashwini Vaishnaw To NDTV | EXCLUSIVE
snippet.description:
US-based semiconductor company Micron Technology is all geared up for setting up India's first chip facility in Gujarat. Micron had announced its decision to set up a semiconductor assembly and test plant in Gujarat during Prime Minister Narendra Modi's state visit to the United States in June. Speaking to NDTV, IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said, "The Micron unit is progressing very well and the land is getting prepared to start the construction unit." "We hope that by December 2024, the first made-in-India memory chip should be shipped out of that plant," he said

snippet.title: $AMAT Applied Materials Inc Q3 2023 Earnings Conference Call
snippet.description:
08/17/2023 Q&A: 20:09
Applied Materials, Inc. engages in the provision of manufacturing equipment, services, and software to the semiconductor, display, and related industries. It operates through three segments: Semiconductor Systems, Applied Global Services, and Display and Adjacent Markets. The Semiconductor Systems segment develops, manufactures, and sells various manufacturing equipment that is used to fabricate semiconductor chips or integrated circuits. This segment also offers various technologies, including epitaxy, ion implantation, oxidation/nitridation, rapid thermal processing, physical vapor deposition, chemical vapor deposition, chemical mechanical planarization, electrochemical deposition, atomic layer deposition, etching, and selective deposition and removal, as well as metrology and inspection tools. The Applied Global Services segment provides integrated solutions to optimize equipment and fab performance and productivity comprising spares, upgrades, services, remanufactured earlier generation equipment, and factory automation software for semiconductor, display, and other products. The Display and Adjacent Markets segment offers products for manufacturing liquid crystal displays; organic light-emitting diodes; and other display technologies for TVs, monitors, laptops, personal computers, electronic tablets, smart phones, and other consumer-oriented devices. The company operates in the United States, China, Korea, Taiwan, Japan, Southeast Asia, and Europe. Applied Materials, Inc. was incorporated in 1967 and is headquartered in Santa Clara, California.
